contract.description: Focus on mistakes, patterns, or non-obvious insights.

selection.selected_title: Are AI Agents Your Next Security Nightmare? (What Everyone’s Missing About ‘Autonomy’)
selection.selected_reason: Strongest fit for authority because it sits exactly at the intersection of LLMs, agents, and real-world deployment risk. It allows you to be slightly contrarian (most people focus on model safety, not agent security), share concrete patterns you’ve seen in tools/agents orchestration, and highlight non-obvious failure modes: over-trusting tool outputs, insecure action spaces, and brittle guardrail assumptions. Very carousel-friendly: each slide can be one specific mistake pattern or design anti-pattern with a tradeoff.
backup_titles: The Hidden Cost of ‘Free’ GenAI Courses: How They Train You to Build Toy Demos, Not Systems
Method Chaining for Data Pipelines: The One Pattern That Actually Survives When You Add LLMs and Agents
caution_notes: Avoid generic ‘AI security is important’ framing; focus on specific agent patterns: tool calling, long-running workflows, memory, and multi-agent setups.
Anchor at least one slide in a real scenario (even anonymized): e.g., an agent with read–write access to a production system, or a retrieval-augmented agent that can exfiltrate data via its tools.
Include at least one counterintuitive point, e.g., that many ‘security’ issues are actually product design failures (unbounded actions, unclear blast radius) rather than traditional infosec bugs.
